3. Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia→Denver, April 12–14): Assigned to Javier Morales. Move dates shifted +1 day from original April 11–13 — confirmed with Patricia Langford. ~2-week Denver temp storage required (lease starts May 1). Storage quote from Javier pending.

body:
Fatimah Al-Rashidi (Relocation Coordinator) completed initial visa and customs review for Dr. Renata Kovacs move (Nashville, TN → Toronto, ON, April 14–17).

Key findings:
1. TN visa (USMCA, Scientist category) lapses on departure — no active US cancellation required.
2. Canopy Health employer letter required (end of US employment + new Canadian role confirmed). Requested from Patricia Langford — deadline April 7.
3. Degree verification copy required for customs broker records — requested from Canopy HR.
4. BorderEase Customs to be engaged as customs broker. Quote expected April 4 — estimated surcharge $700–$900.
5. Heartland Movers flagged as unsuitable for this cross-border move (no international customs experience). Swift Relocations being evaluated as alternative.
6. MacBook Pro (~$2,800 CAD) will be individually declared on BSF186 — duty-free under settler's exemption.
7. 4 lab notebooks with proprietary research notes: advised Dr. Kovacs to carry personally (not in shipment). Canopy Legal may need to advise.

Blocker: If employer letter not received by April 7, April 14 pickup date at risk — may need to shift 1–2 days.
title: Dr. Kovacs Move — Visa/Customs Risk Assessment (Fatimah Al-Rashidi)
description: Coordinator risk assessment for Dr. Kovacs international relocation — visa documentation and Canadian customs broker requirements.

body:

Heartland confirmed via phone (Jake Loomis) — standard insulated trucks only, unsuitable for biomedical cryogenic cargo.

Split-vendor arrangement approved by Chloe Vance (Ops Manager):
- Swift Relocations: specialized cold-chain freight for 3 cryogenic items (2× Thermo CryoPlus 3800, 1× MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask) — delivery to Canopy Health Chicago lab receiving dock
- Heartland Movers: standard household goods (~300 cu ft) — delivery to Marcus Webb's Chicago apartment

Outstanding items:
- Swift quote pending (requested April 3, needed by April 5)
- Chicago apartment address for Marcus Webb — Catalina to obtain from client
- Chicago lab receiving dock address — Catalina to obtain from Patricia Langford
- Canopy Health documentation for biological samples (chain of custody) — Swift may require

Cold-chain freight surcharge to be added to Canopy Health invoice once Swift quote confirmed.

body:
Julian Brooks took an inbound support call from Emilia Cruz around 10:05 AM PT on April 12.

Caller was upset and sounded embarrassed that the scratch on her Steinway had become visible to neighbors and building staff during the move. She said she had already seen the vendor photos and wanted a straight answer on whether MoveOps was going to take responsibility for the damage.

Key points from call:
Emilia said the piano was in good condition before extraction.
She described the scratch as obvious and unacceptable given the move was presented as white-glove handling.
She requested a callback the same day from someone who could speak to next steps, liability, and repair coverage.
She asked specifically, "Is MoveOps actually taking responsibility here or am I going to get bounced between vendors?"

Julian told her the concern would be logged immediately with the relocation team and account coverage for same-day follow-up.

No owner assigned during call log entry.
title: Inbound complaint call from Emilia Cruz about piano damage

body:
April 2026 Relocation Wave — 3 New Active Moves + Significant Challenges

This is the most intricate set of relocations we've received from Vectral Systems so far. Three relocations occurring concurrently, each with unique challenges that necessitated additional fees and special coordination. Summary below.

**1. Priya Suresh (Senior Data Engineer) — Portland, OR → Denver, CO | April 14–18**
- Pet arrangements needed: Priya is moving with her emotional support German Shepherd, Atlas (85 lbs, ESA documentation available). The new housing must be pet-friendly with a fenced yard or nearby dog park — the landlord must explicitly allow dogs over 85 lbs. Preferred areas: RiNo (top choice) or Highlands.
- Pet Relocation Coordination Fee applied ($350).
- Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations.

**2. Terrence Nakamura (Staff Platform Architect) — Austin, TX → San Francisco, CA | April 16–22**
- ADA-compliant housing needed: Wheelchair user since 2019. Requires a roll-in shower, doorways of at least 36 inches, and either ground floor or building with an elevator. Preferred neighborhoods in SF: SoMa or Mission Bay, near BART/Muni.
- Split-shipment necessary: Estimated household weight of 9,200 lbs exceeds the standard single-truck capacity. Two vendor legs arranged — Heartland Movers handling the main household items (~6,000 lbs, Shipment 1) and Swift Relocations managing the overflow (~3,200 lbs, Shipment 2) which includes a 150 lb server rack (sensitive equipment, padded crating needed) and around 40 boxes of books.
- ADA Housing Sourcing Premium ($400) + Split-Shipment Fee ($900) applied.
- **Precedent note:** This split-shipment for the Nakamura relocation is the first for Vectral. Considering their engineering team frequently has heavy home-office and lab equipment, this may set a standard for future Vectral moves. It’s worth discussing with operations whether to incorporate split-shipment into Vectral's standard offerings or maintain it as an additional fee.

**3. Camille Oduya (Principal Product Manager) — Boston, MA → Seattle, WA | April 12–21**
- Tight schedule: Boston lease must be vacated by April 12 (fixed date), Seattle office starts April 21 — only 9 days for a cross-country relocation. Movers need to pick up on April 12; household items must reach Seattle by April 16. Vectral has already secured the Seattle apartment (1422 Minor Ave, Apt 7B) — no apartment search needed on our part, just flight + movers. **Priority scheduling required.**
- Enhanced stipend exception: Vectral has arranged a $3,500 stipend for home-office equipment for Camille (as opposed to the standard $2,000) as part of her offer. This needs to be set up in the stipend platform before her arrival on April 14. Alerting finance/operations to ensure this is completed on time.

**Financials:** Invoice INV-2026-0410 issued, total $15,900. Due 2026-05-10.

**Account context:** This raises Vectral's total active relocations to 5 (Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei from the previous wave are still in process). The account is expanding in both volume and complexity.
title: April 2026 Relocation Wave — 3 New Active Relocations + Notable Complications
description: Account note: 3 new Vectral Systems relocations (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya) with pet, ADA/split-shipment, and enhanced stipend complications. Most complex batch from this client to date.

body:
Account Update — GreenStack Energy — April 2026 Relocations (Zhao + Reyes)

Two new complex relocations received from Derek Hollis (People Ops Manager) on April 1, 2026.

RELOCATION 1: Mei-Lin Zhao — Senior Battery Chemist — Houston → Portland
- HAZMAT component: ~350 lbs lithium-ion battery test modules (DOT Class 9, UN3481-certified). Climate-controlled transport required (15–25°C). Must ship separately from household goods on certified hazmat carrier.
- TIMING: Houston lab lease hard-ends April 17. Portland lab buildout delayed to April 28 (was April 20). 11-day interim storage gap requires DOT-compliant, temperature-controlled facility in Portland metro area. GreenStack covers storage costs.
- VENDOR: Swift Relocations assigned (has Class 9 DOT-certified drivers). Chloe confirming April 17 pickup availability.
- STORAGE LEAD: Pacific Crest Logistics (Rivergate, Portland) — only known compliant facility. Requires 10 business days advance notice — timeline is extremely tight.

RELOCATION 2: Joaquín Reyes — Solar Systems Integration Lead — Phoenix → Seattle
- SPECIALTY EQUIPMENT: 900-lb rooftop solar testing rig (8ft × 4ft × 3ft). Needs padded custom crating, flatbed transport, freight elevator + loading dock at GreenStack Seattle HQ (401 Terry Ave N, north side).
- SCHEDULING CONFLICT: GreenStack Seattle office shutdown April 14–18 for electrical grid upgrades. Zero building access during this window. Rig delivery must complete by EOD April 13 OR be scheduled for April 19 at 10:00 AM or later.
- VENDOR CONCERN: Heartland Movers initially assigned but may not be rated for 900-lb equipment. Chloe recommends checking if Swift can handle this leg instead.

PENDING ITEMS:
1. Interim hazmat storage — $1,650 line item on invoice awaiting Derek Hollis approval
2. Coordinator assignments from Chloe Vance (expected EOD April 1)
3. Hazmat transport surcharge rate validation — $1,800 is estimated; MoveOps has no prior Class 9 hazmat benchmark
4. Heartland vs. Swift decision for Reyes rig delivery

INVOICE: INV-2026-0418, $9,440 total. DEADLINE: Must be finalized by April 25 per GreenStack fiscal year-end.

— Emeka Diallo, Account Manager
title: GreenStack Energy — April 2026 Relocations (Zhao + Reyes) — Complex Moves Summary
description: Comprehensive account update covering two new GreenStack Energy relocations with hazmat transport, interim storage, building shutdown conflict, and overweight specialty equipment challenges.

body:
PivotPoint Labs — April 2026 Dual Relocation Summary (Okonjo + Castañeda)

Sam Delgado (Co-founder & CEO) emailed April 8 requesting two new relocations, both to Austin. This brings PivotPoint's total relocations with MoveOps to 4 (Ines Kovac completed, Sasha Greenwald in progress, plus these 2 new). This is their most complex batch yet and their highest single invoice at $10,650.

**Email Thread Summary:**
- April 8, 9:15am: Sam sends initial request with detailed requirements for both moves.
- April 8, 11:42am: I (Catalina) replied confirming we can handle both. Flagged Fatimah Al-Rashidi for Nadia's visa coordination. Mentioned Swift Relocations for Diego's CNC crating. Quoted walkup surcharge range of $400–600.
- April 8, 2:28pm: Sam followed up with addresses, building restrictions, and logistics details. Key items: Nadia's building super requires 48-hr notice and weekdays-only moves; Diego wants a pre-move crating inspection (April 10–11); PivotPoint's FY ends April 30 so they need the invoice dated before then.

**Key Complications & Risks:**
1. H-1B Visa Timing (Nadia): Her visa expires June 15, 2026. USCIS address-change filing MUST happen before physical move. Attorney Lorraine Pham (Pham & Associates) is expecting our outreach. If filing is delayed, move date slips — tight window.
2. CNC Mill Freight (Diego): 600-lb Tormach PCNC 440 is a first for MoveOps in terms of precision industrial equipment. Requires specialty crating, climate transport, and high-value cargo insurance. Sets a potential precedent for future 'specialty equipment relocation' service offerings.
3. Storage Gap (Diego): 16-day gap between Brooklyn lease end (April 15) and Austin apartment availability (May 1). Climate-controlled storage with dock access needed. Chloe Vance checking SecureStore Austin availability.
4. Pre-Move Inspection (Diego): Employee wants to personally supervise CNC crating — need to coordinate specific time window April 10–11 before the April 12–15 pickup window.

**Financials:**
- Invoice INV-2026-0315 issued April 9, 2026 (dated per client FY-end request). Due May 9 (Net 30).
- Standard packages: 2 × $3,520 = $7,040
- Surcharges: Walkup ($500) + Custom Crating ($1,200) + Climate Freight ($850) + Storage 16 days ($640) + Cargo Insurance ($420) = $3,610
- Total: $10,650

**Coordinator Assignments:**
- Nadia Okonjo → Fatimah Al-Rashidi (visa experience), vendor: Swift Relocations
- Diego Castañeda → Javier Morales (heavy equipment documentation), vendor: Heartland Movers

**Account Observations:**
Sam Delgado continues to be very hands-on — providing detailed specs, addresses, building restrictions, and even equipment insurance values upfront. Trust level with MoveOps is growing. PivotPoint is a 32-person startup but increasingly relying on us for complex, high-margin moves. Worth monitoring as a potential case study for specialty equipment relocations.
title: PivotPoint Labs — April 2026 Dual Relocation (Okonjo + Castañeda) — Account Update & Email Thread Summary
description: Account note: 2 new PivotPoint Labs relocations with H-1B visa coordination, heavy CNC equipment crating, 16-day storage gap, and pre-move inspection requirements. Highest single invoice ($10,650) from this client to date.

body:

Mosaic Robotics submitted 3 new relocation requests on April 1, 2026, via Tamara Byrd (HR Manager). This brings Mosaic's total active relocations to 6 — the most of any MoveOps client this quarter.

**Active Mosaic relocations as of April 2, 2026:**
1. Andre Washington — Detroit → Atlanta (core_clients era, completed)
2. Lena Kowalski — → Salt Lake City (active_relocations, in progress)
3. Andre Mitchell — → Boise (active_relocations, in progress)
4. Zara Okoye — Savannah → Atlanta (NEW, in progress) — $2,700 deal, Heartland Movers, coord: Blessing Okafor
5. Deshawn Hartley — Tampa → Denver (NEW, in progress) — $1,520 deal, Swift Relocations, coord: Suki Patel
6. Keiko Tanaka — Columbus → Seattle (NEW, in progress) — $2,770 deal, Swift Relocations, coord: Javier Morales

**Key flags:**
- $2,450 in non-standard surcharges across Okoye ($1,200 prototype crating) and Tanaka ($800 hazmat + $450 storage) — pending Tamara's written approval
- Deshawn Hartley has a hard April 19 delivery deadline (Automate 2026 trade show, Denver Convention Center, April 21–24)
- Keiko Tanaka has an 8-day lease gap (April 15–23) requiring interim storage at Swift's Seattle facility

🔵 DUAL-CLIENT TRANSPARENCY NOTE: Deshawn Hartley's partner is Simone Richter, Data Platform Lead at BrightLoop Analytics. Simone is currently being relocated by MoveOps (Chicago → Boston, rush job, managed by Mina Hashimoto). Per Tamara Byrd: no coordination needed between the two moves — they are moving independently to different cities for their respective employers. Possible consolidated move to Denver in Q3/Q4 2026. Chloe Vance (Ops Manager) confirmed this does not rise to the level of a formal COI disclosure per MoveOps policy. This note is logged for transparency and future reference. If a consolidated move request comes in later, we will have documentation of when the connection was first identified.

Invoice: INV-2026-0411 — $9,010 total. Account Manager: Emeka Diallo.

body:
Account Recovery Context -- NorthWind Technologies Denver Satellite Office Expansion

NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind) has requested 2 new employee relocations as part of opening a Denver satellite office. This is a significant moment for the account relationship.

BACKGROUND:
NorthWind signed a $60,000 contract in January 2026 for 5 enterprise relocations at $12K each. Execution was poor: two Phase 1 apartments failed quality standards (HVAC malfunction in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), and my response time to Pam Kowalski's complaint was unacceptably slow. Pam escalated directly to David Chen (Customer Engagement Lead) citing unresponsive account management and quality concerns. David personally intervened and committed to service improvements. NorthWind's Q1 satisfaction survey came back at 5/10 overall, 4/10 on communication quality, 5/10 on service quality.

Despite this history, Pam Kowalski (Director of HR Operations) has come back to MoveOps for the Denver expansion. She explicitly stated in her email: "NorthWind is giving you a second chance here and I expect better service this time." Victor Huang (CISO) also expressed skepticism, writing that he is "not fully comfortable delegating this to a relocation vendor we've had quality issues with."

CURRENT REQUEST:
1) Raj Patel (Principal Architect) -- Chicago to Denver, week of April 21. Server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers, lithium UPS batteries (hazmat Class 9). CISO requires: chain-of-custody log (signed, timestamped), IT security escort supervision during packing/loading, direct transport only, real-time GPS tracking. This chain-of-custody protocol is unprecedented for MoveOps -- we are developing the template and process from scratch while executing it for a skeptical CISO. Assigned to Swift Relocations.

2) Emilia Cruz (Director of Marketing) -- Chicago to Denver, hard deadline April 18. Steinway Model B grand piano requiring specialty subcontractor (Gentle Giant Piano Movers, no existing vendor agreement). Three-vendor coordination. 27-day lease overlap with early termination negotiation requested (outside MoveOps standard scope). Assigned to Heartland Movers (household goods).

RISKS:
- Chain-of-custody is a completely new service -- no template, no prior experience, unknown whether Swift will accept supervised loading terms
- Piano subcontractor (Gentle Giant) has no vendor agreement, no insurance verification, no rate sheet -- onboarding a new vendor for a high-stakes account
- Lease negotiation is outside standard scope -- unclear if MoveOps should offer this or refer to a tenant's rights attorney
- Invoice at $14,700 ($7,350/move avg) is below original $12K/move enterprise rate -- pricing may need revision
- Two invoice line items are provisional and pending vendor confirmation

OPPORTUNITY:
If we execute well on both relocations, we recover the NorthWind relationship and position MoveOps for additional satellite office moves. The chain-of-custody capability could also become a differentiating service line for tech companies relocating employees with proprietary equipment. NorthWind has 150 employees and is actively expanding -- this is a growth account if we can earn back their trust.

Coordinator: Blessing Okafor (both moves)
Account Manager: Catalina Dubois
CE Lead oversight: David Chen (cc'd on all communications)
title: NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Expansion Account Recovery (2 Relocations, April 2026)
description: Comprehensive account recovery note documenting the context, risks, and opportunity for NorthWind's 2 new Denver satellite office relocations. Critical account with troubled Q1 history (5/10 CSAT, escalation to CE Lead). Chain-of-custody protocol is unprecedented for MoveOps.

body:
DAMAGE INCIDENT LOG -- Zara Okoye Relocation, Mosaic Robotics
Logged by: Emeka Diallo (Account Manager) on April 15, 2026, 5:45 PM PT

INCIDENT OVERVIEW:
Robotic arm prototype (Unit #1, $90K value) arrived damaged at Atlanta destination following Savannah-to-Atlanta move coordinated by MoveOps on April 14-15, 2026. Damage includes cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment. Move was handled by Heartland Movers under work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, coordinated by Blessing Okafor.

CHAIN OF CUSTODY TIMELINE:
April 14, 7:30 AM -- Heartland crew (crew lead: Marcus Delaney) arrives at 1247 Abercorn St, Apt 3B, Savannah, GA for packing. Work order specified 4-inch minimum anti-vibration polyethylene foam on all faces.
April 14, ~9:45 AM -- Blessing Okafor countersigns packing checklist REMOTELY via digital signature. She was physically in Philadelphia coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's Canopy Health move (Philadelphia to Denver) and did not visually verify crating materials or foam density.
April 14, ~10:00 AM -- Loading completed. Two Pelican cases + control unit on Heartland truck (designated climate-controlled, 60-80F).
April 14-15 -- Transit via I-16 West to I-75 North (~250 mi). Overnight stop or depot transfer status unconfirmed.
April 15, 9:15 AM -- Delivery at Atlanta address. Receipt signed by Kyle Britten (Mosaic Robotics intern). Zara Okoye was at Mosaic Atlanta office for orientation. No inspection performed at time of delivery.
April 15, ~1:00 PM -- Zara returns, inspects prototypes. Discovers cracked servo housing on Unit #1 (3-inch fracture, wiring harness exposed) and 4+ degree calibration drift on two axes.
April 15, 1:28 PM -- Zara emails Tamara Byrd internally reporting damage, CC's Rafael Mendes.
April 15, 3:42 PM -- Tamara Byrd emails Emeka Diallo (MoveOps) formally escalating. Demands incident report and damage assessment within 48 hours. Rafael Mendes CC'd.
April 15, 4:14 PM -- Emeka acknowledges and commits to 48-hour deadline. CC's Chloe Vance (MoveOps Ops Manager).

KEY FINDINGS:
1. CRATING VERIFICATION GAP: Coordinator Blessing Okafor was managing two same-day pack-and-load operations in Savannah and Philadelphia simultaneously. She signed off on Heartland's packing checklist remotely based on verbal confirmation from crew lead Marcus Delaney, without physically verifying foam density against the 4-inch minimum spec. Blessing has acknowledged this was a procedural failure on her part and has offered to provide her full packing supervision log.
2. UNAUTHORIZED DELIVERY RECIPIENT: Delivery was accepted and signed for by Kyle Britten, a Mosaic Robotics intern, not by Zara Okoye (the authorized recipient). This creates a ~3.75-hour gap between delivery and inspection during which damage provenance cannot be conclusively established. Heartland's insurance adjusters could use this to challenge the claim.
3. INSURANCE COVERAGE GAP (flagged by Marcus Thorne, Head of Finance): MoveOps' vendor liability policy covers $50,000 per item maximum. The damaged unit is valued at $90,000. This creates a $40,000 uncovered gap representing MoveOps' direct financial exposure if Heartland does not accept liability.

FINANCIAL IMPACT:
- Damaged prototype value: $90,000
- Vendor liability coverage: $50,000
- MoveOps direct exposure: $40,000 (worst case without Heartland acceptance)
- QuickBooks credit memo: CM-2026-0415 (against INV-2026-0411)
- QuickBooks liability accrual: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90K contingent)
- Revenue at risk: $9,010 (full Mosaic April invoice) + ongoing account relationship

ACCOUNT RELATIONSHIP STATUS: AT RISK
Mosaic Robotics is a new client (~2 months). The damaged equipment is part of an active DARPA-adjacent R&D program. VP of Engineering Rafael Mendes is now directly engaged. The incident report is due EOD April 17 and must include full transparency about MoveOps' procedural failures (remote crating sign-off) and the $40K insurance gap.

NEXT STEPS:
1. Chloe Vance compiling final incident timeline with Heartland transit records
2. Incident report to be sent to Tamara Byrd by EOD April 17
3. Marcus Thorne scheduling call with MoveOps insurance broker re: claim process
4. Emeka to approach Heartland Movers re: cost-sharing on uncovered $40K
5. $1,200 crating surcharge billing hold recommended by Marcus pending claim resolution

Related deal: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026
title: DAMAGE INCIDENT -- Prototype Unit #1 ($90K) -- Zara Okoye Relocation -- $40K insurance gap, account at risk
description: Critical damage incident engagement logged by Emeka Diallo documenting the damaged robotic arm prototype during Zara Okoye's Savannah-to-Atlanta relocation. Captures full chain-of-custody timeline, coordinator verification gap, intern-signed delivery receipt complication, and $40K insurance coverage shortfall. Account relationship flagged as at risk.

body:
Vectral Q3 2026 renewal negotiation -- status update.

Final offer delivered to Rachel Whitfield (VP of People) on April 19. Holding at 10% loyalty discount on relocation services via the Preferred Partner Program. Rachel originally demanded 15% across all services, then counter-proposed 12% on relocation only. We held firm at 10% per CEO directive and added three service sweeteners at no cost: dedicated onboarding coordinator per employee, 48-hour response SLA, and quarterly account reviews.

Rachel has accepted the 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, and $2K/month stipend platform fee. The only unresolved point is the discount percentage -- she wanted 12%, we offered 10% with the service enhancements.

Next step: Rachel presents vendor renewal recommendations to Vectral board on April 23. We should have a final answer by end of that week.

Risk assessment: Medium. She has committed to every structural term and the tone of her emails has been constructive throughout. The 2% gap ($720/year) is unlikely to be a dealbreaker given the service sweeteners. Primary risk is if a competitor makes an aggressive last-minute offer before the board meeting.
title: Q3 2026 Preferred Partner Renewal -- Final Offer Delivered, Awaiting Board Decision
description: Negotiation log for Vectral Systems contract renewal. Final offer: 10% relocation discount + service enhancements. Board decision expected April 23.

body:
INCIDENT LOG — Reply All Pricing Leak (April 22, 2026)

On April 22, Catalina Dubois accidentally Reply-All'd an internal pricing strategy email to Rachel Nguyen (VP People Ops, Terraform Digital). The email was intended for the internal pricing discussion thread but was sent to Julian Brooks' forward chain, which had Rachel CC'd.

Information exposed to client:
- MoveOps' 25% margin floor (described as "non-negotiable")
- Tiered volume discount structure: standard for 1-10 moves, 8% at 11-20, 12% at 21+
- Internal assessment that Terraform would likely push for 15-20% discount
- Discussion about whether to present pricing proactively vs. reactively

Rachel's response: Professional but firm. She immediately locked in the tiered discount structure as her expectation and noted she'd "learned more about MoveOps' pricing from an accidental email than from any deliberate communication." She also flagged that she still did not have a named AM at that point.

Resolution actions taken:
1. Catalina sent personal apology to Rachel within 1 hour
2. Elena Rostova assigned Mina Hashimoto as dedicated AM (best margin track record, zero escalations)
3. Elena authorized hiring a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator
4. Pricing decision: honor tiered structure Rachel saw — lead with 8% tier for 11-20 moves; do NOT proactively offer 12% tier
5. Mina sent professional introduction email same day; Rachel responded next morning with full relocation details for 3 employees

Current status: Relationship stabilized. Rachel is engaged and providing relocation details. Monitoring closely — Rachel indicated she is "watching closely" to ensure execution matches intention.

Logged by: Mina Hashimoto, Account Manager
title: Reply All Incident — Internal Pricing Discussion Leaked to Client
description: Critical client relationship incident requiring CEO intervention. Accidental disclosure of internal margin targets and volume discount strategy to Terraform Digital's VP of People Operations. Relationship stabilized through rapid response — apology, AM assignment, and coordinator hire authorization.

body:
CRM updated by Mina Hashimoto (AM) on 2026-04-23 to reflect new executive relocation for Canopy Health.

KEY UPDATES:
1. New CRM Contact added: Dr. Anand Mehta, Chief Medical Officer (incoming), Canopy Health. Email: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io. Currently based in Boston, MA. Relocating to Chicago, IL.
2. New Deal created: 'Canopy Health — Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)' | Amount: $12,170 | Stage: In Progress | Close date: May 7, 2026.
3. QuickBooks estimate EST-2026-0422 issued for $12,170, flagged as priority billing, Net-15 terms.

ACCOUNT CONTEXT:
Canopy Health has been with MoveOps since July 2024 — our longest-standing client. This is the FIRST C-suite relocation they've entrusted to us. All previous Canopy relocations were mid-level staff:
- Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Clinical Product Lead, Minneapolis to Chicago) — completed
- Lily Marchetti (UX Researcher, Nashville to Chicago) — completed/cancelled
- Rachel Dominguez (Clinical Data Analyst, LA to Portland) — active
- Kevin Okafor (Health Systems Coordinator, Atlanta to Portland) — active
- Dr. Renata Kovacs (Senior Research Scientist, Nashville to Toronto) — active, international
- Marcus Webb (Lab Research Associate, Detroit to Chicago) — active
- Tyler Okonkwo (Product Ops Manager, Philadelphia to Denver) — active

Moving to executive-level placements represents a significant upgrade in Canopy's trust level. Patricia Langford (Dir. of HR) bypassed the usual account manager (Catalina Dubois) and came directly to me for this one, citing the need for VIP-level attention.

REVENUE IMPACT:
- This single relocation at $12,170 is approximately 3x the standard Canopy relocation value (~$3,000-$4,000).
- Canopy's cumulative lifetime value with MoveOps now includes the original 5-relocation onboarding deal, the Q2 2026 wave (INV-2026-0401, $9,210), active Portland relocations, and now this executive package.
- If we execute well, David Chen believes this opens the door to future C-suite moves at this price point.

NEXT STEPS:
- Dr. Mehta selecting temporary housing (3 UrbanNest options presented, awaiting decision by April 25)
- Chain-of-custody documentation template to be obtained from Canopy compliance team
- Swift Relocations to be engaged for Boston-Chicago household move
- Estimate EST-2026-0422 to be sent to Patricia Langford for approval
title: Canopy Health — Executive Relocation (Dr. Anand Mehta, CMO) — First C-Suite Placement, $12,170 Estimate
description: Account update note documenting Canopy Health's first executive-level relocation with MoveOps. New CRM contact, deal, and QuickBooks estimate created. Strategic significance flagged by CE Lead.
